mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend growth of 150,000 per month signals robust labor market strength and consumer spending capacity, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth averages below 100,000 over any 3-month period while SPX still produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units in the same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to predict negative returns with directional accuracy > 55% in any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding productive capacity and manufacturing strength, supporting positive equity valuations over 5 trading days as supply-side constraints ease and profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations contract and recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration leading to recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict negative returns with directional accuracy > 52% in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) expanding above 1,400 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic optimism, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction employment and consumer confidence rise.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1,400 thousand units do not predict positive 60-day SPX returns with directional accuracy > 54%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.0%) signals stagflationary pressure and supply-side weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 59% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to achieve directional accuracy > 53% in any 18-month rolling window.
